rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AIKO ONODERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/9/23 for an appearance. (No earlier available date.) Some of the issues to be addressed at the hearing: incomplete accounting information; "reimbursements" to Hiroki Onodera without any support; incomplete descriptions for some disbursements (e.g., what were accountants paid for and why was Elizabeth reimbursed for "payroll"?); the incorrect balance in the Bank of America account without an amendment to the accounting; and the addition of another Bank of America checking account to assets on hand that is reportedly not an estate account. It appears that bank statements are pending e-filing. They must also be accompanied by a statement by the personal representative or attorney if they are computer-generated statements. [CRC 7.575(b)and LRSF 14.34 D.] (NB: Court-conformed accountings are cash accountings. Reinvested dividends should be shown on an informational schedule, not the receipts schedule.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SY J. RUSSELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/9/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: A brokerage account does not usually contain all cash. Please file the brokerage account statement immediately preceding the date of death as confirmation. If the brokerage account was not all cash, the holdings must be appraised by the Probate Referee. Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. The trustee of the devisee trust must waive the accounting, not (just) the trust beneficiaries. A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (There were personal property taxes paid by the estate?) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/2/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CECILIA P BLAKE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Letters Of Administration
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/04/2023, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 09/19/2022: 1) No proposed order (DE-140 form) has been received. 2) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form. 3) No proof of publication is on file. 4) Item 2b of the petition: The name of the proposed personal representative must be specified. 5) Item 2d: At least one box must be checked. Please note that the Court requires a minimum bond of $20,000 for a nonresident of California, regardless of a waiver. A declaration required by LRSF 14.16(F) must be filed to disclose the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate. 6) Item 3d must be completed. Please also provide brief descriptions of the assets subject to this probate. 7) Item 5b: At least one box must be checked. 8) Item 8: Jordan Blake's relationship to the decedent must be specified. If he is waiving the petitioner's bond, the waiver must be filed on the DE-142 form. An appearance may be required on 01/04/2023 on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar for lack of progress. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
